blocks.content: Thomas Hailey answered on the third ring.
His voice warm. Easy. The voice of a man with no reason to expect anything other than an ordinary call on an ordinary morning.
The silence lasted only a second. But Thomas heard it. After all those years of carrying the quiet fear in the background of every good day, he heard the thing in that silence.
Different word now. Same name, different weight. A father who has heard something in the quiet that he has been dreading since the day a doctor sat across from him and changed everything.

He heard Thomas breathe. In. Out. The steadying breath of a man who has been preparing for something his whole life and finds, now that it has arrived, that all that preparation has not helped at all.
Quietly. Carefully. The voice of someone who needs to hear it even though hearing it will break him open.
The sound Thomas Hailey made was not a word.
